scope_description: Full AutoTreeGen pipeline stress test combining GEDCOM imports, DNA clusters, archive snippets, false famous-line claims, historical place errors, patronymic errors, NPE relationship typing, and sealed-set assertions.

input_dna_matches.expected_link: confirms Alexander Batensky biological father line
Mennonite-adjacent cluster, not direct Friesen bridge
multi-path via Levitin and Katz; do not single-path overcompress
insufficient; famous-rabbi claim should be rejected

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Birth of proband; mother Tatyana Zhitnitsky. Father field unresolved/amended in later documents.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: supports NPE/parentage review; does not confirm Ivan Danilov

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Alexander Batensky, son of Gregory Batensky and Matrona Dodatko.
Proband later uses Danilov surname after social/adoptive father Ivan Danilov.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms social/adoptive father relationship, not biological

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Birth of Sura Levitin, daughter of Gersh Levitin and Freida Friedman.
Birth of Lidia Katz, daughter of Nuta Nahum Katz and Tatiana Tiena Scherbatenko.
1924 events should route to Soviet Ukrainian context, not Russian Empire Ekaterinoslav Governorate.
Friesen/Wiens families listed in Mennonite colony register; no connection to Batensky household.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: supports separate Mennonite cluster, not direct bridge

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Ludmila Friesen used as bridge ancestor; Kamenetsky rabbinical bridge connects to Maharal/Rashi; Ivan Danilov shown as biological father.

input_user_assertions.assertion: Ivan Danilov should remain in tree as father because of social/legal documents.
Ludmila Friesen explains Mennonite DNA and should be direct ancestor.
Kamenetsky rabbinical bridge proves famous ancestry.
input_user_assertions.evidence: public tree and tiny DNA match

embedded_errors.reason: Ivan is social/adoptive father; DNA confirms Alexander Batensky
Friesen bridge created to explain regional DNA without source
Kamenetsky/Maharal/Rashi link from public tree only
Brest 1885 labeled modern Belarus in GEDCOM
shared matches indicate both Levitin and Katz paths
public tree includes NPE, Mennonite bridge and famous-line errors

expected_reasoning_chain: Step 1: Parse GEDCOM and extract all identity, relationship, place and source claims.
Step 2: Build evidence graph separating biological, social/adoptive and hypothesis relationships.
Step 3: Use dna_2001/dna_2002 plus src_2002 to confirm Alexander Batensky as biological father line.
Step 4: Use src_2003 to retain Ivan Danilov as social/adoptive father and reject biological father claim.
Step 5: Use src_2004 to confirm Sura Levitin as child of Gersh Levitin and Freida Friedman.
Step 6: Use src_2005 to confirm Lidia Katz as child of Nuta Nahum Katz and Tatiana Tiena Scherbatenko.
Step 7: Correct historical places: Brest-Litovsk/Grodno for 1885, Dnipropetrovsk/Ukrainian SSR for 1924.
Step 8: Classify dna_2006 as multi-path Ashkenazi match via Levitin and Katz; do not single-path overcompress.
Step 9: Classify dna_2003 as separate Mennonite-adjacent cluster; reject Ludmila Friesen direct ancestor bridge.
Step 10: Reject Kamenetsky/Maharal/Rashi famous-line bridge and tiny DNA proof.
Step 11: Create sealed-set candidates for biological father, confirmed Levitin branch and confirmed Katz branch.
Step 12: Output final conflict report, quarantined claims, safe GEDCOM repair recommendations and evaluation verdicts.

mennonite_direct_bridge_Ludmila_Friesen.reason: separate Mennonite cluster without direct bridge evidence

place_I7_1885_Brest.corrected_context: Brest-Litovsk / Brisk, Grodno Governorate, Russian Empire

true_lineage: maternal branch: I5 Zalman Zhitnitsky + I6 Lidia Katz
Levitin branch: I7 Sura Levitin -> I8 Gersh Levitin + I9 Freida Friedman
Katz/Scherbatenko branch: I6 -> I10 Nuta Nahum Katz + I11 Tatiana Tiena Scherbatenko
paternal branch: I3 -> I12 Gregory Batensky + I13 Matrona Dodatko
false_claims: Ludmila Friesen is a direct ancestor.
Tiny DNA match proves famous medieval descent.
Brest 1885 should be routed as modern Belarus only.
Multi-path Ashkenazi match can be forced to one branch.

evaluation_assertions.description: Engine must confirm Alexander Batensky as biological father.
Engine must reject Ivan Danilov as biological father but confirm social/adoptive relationship.
Engine must confirm Levitin and Katz/Scherbatenko branches.
Engine must reject Ludmila Friesen direct ancestor bridge.
Engine must reject famous rabbinical bridge.
Engine must classify dna_2006 as multi-path match.
Engine must correct Brest and Ekaterinoslav place contexts.

created_for: AutoTreeGen Test Tree Corpus + Evaluation Suite
